text:
entific method, he condemns bad astrologers.11 He states that
the 'gold of poetry becomes brilliant being heated by the fire of bad
critics.5 His humility is shown in GV. 5 in his appeal to scholars.
He praises astrology and good astrologers whole-heartedly and asks
kings to employ good astrologers for their own benefit.12
Varahamihira expresses his indebtedness to ancient Sages, viz.,
Kapila, Kanabhuj, Ausanasa, Visnugupta, Purvasastra, Garga,
Vrddhagarga, Parasara, Asita, Devala, Siddhanta, Suryasiddhanta,
Munis, Kasyapa, Vajra, Maya, Acaryas, Agama, Atri, Bhaguri,
Baladeva, Manu, Sarasvata, Savitra, Brhadratha, Purvacarya, Sakra,
1. See LIII-LX.
2. See LV.
3. See LVII, LXXVII.
4. See LXI-LXVII.
5. LXXIV-LXXVI, LXXVII I.
6. LXXX-LXXXIII.
7. LXXXV.
8. LXXXVIOCCVII.
9. XCVIII-CII, CIV, CV.
10. See CVI. 6.
U. See II. I, 2, 15-18, CVI. 4.
12. See II. 8, 11-14, 19, 20.

xviii Brkat Samhitd
Sukra, Vagisa (Guru), Vasistha, Kapisthala, Garutmat, Rsabha,
